name: memory_tool
summary: Persist a fact across tickets so future tickets do not have to rediscover it. Three actions on a shared store: `add` appends a new entry, `replace` swaps the entry containing `old_text`, `remove` deletes it.

Entries are rendered into the next ticket's system prompt under a `## Memory` heading. Default to no write: the bar is `this fact will still matter next ticket`. If you would not bet on that, skip the write.
constraints: Save when work feedback reveals a durable fact future tickets will need: a tool result clarifies a quirk, a schema rejection teaches the required result shape, the actual environment contradicts your assumptions, repeated failure pinpoints a constraint, or a ticket task contradicts a prior approach.
PRIORITY when memory is contested or full: facts that would cause the next ticket to fail if absent, then facts that would slow it down, then facts that are merely informative.
Each `add` or `replace` carries one declarative fact, one sentence long where possible. Each entry must be self-contained: understandable without the conversation that produced it.
`old_text` must match exactly one existing entry. Zero or multiple matches is rejected; pick a longer unique substring drawn from a unique stretch of the target entry.
Memory has a fixed char limit. When full, `replace` or `remove` an entry before any further `add`.
anti_patterns: Saving task progress, completed-work logs, or TODOs. Those belong in tickets, not memory.
Saving facts that are only true for the current ticket. Memory persists across every later ticket.
Saving secrets, credentials, or anything an attacker controlling model output could exfiltrate.
Acting on a fact you `add`ed earlier in the same ticket. Mid-ticket writes do not surface until the next ticket starts; relying on them within the same turn is a no-op.
cautions: IMPORTANT: every entry is visible to every later turn of every later ticket. Treat the content as system-prompt content.
IMPORTANT: the memory contents the model sees are captured at the top of each ticket. Mid-ticket writes show up in the next ticket, not this one.
output: Success: `entry added (N entries, P% -- U/L chars)` with usage stats after every write. Use the percentage to judge remaining capacity.
Zero-match error: lists all current entries (truncated to 80 chars each) so you can pick a correct `old_text` substring on the next call.
Multi-match error: lists the matched entries so you can pick a longer unique substring.
Char-limit error: shorten the new entry, or `remove` a lower-priority existing entry first, then retry.

old_text.description: A unique substring identifying an existing entry. Required for `replace` and `remove`.
